name: control_device
description: Make the user's own phone (or tablet/XR device) perform a task, driven by a CLOUD frontier model — the AI BlackBox itself watches the device screen over Tailscale and actuates it step by step by DRIVING THE ON-SCREEN UI (open apps, read the screen, tap, type, scroll, submit fields). It works the app the way a person would — so 'set a timer', 'look up a place on a map', or 'check a setting' happen by navigating that app's UI, not via a fixed one-shot command. THIS is the tool for 'do X on my phone', 'use my phone to ...', 'have my device open/show/check ...'. It differs from control_phone (which delegates to the SLOWER on-device Gemma): control_device keeps the smart model in the cloud and uses the phone only as hands, so it starts fast (no ~75s model cold-load). YOU MUST ACTUALLY CALL THIS TOOL AND WAIT FOR ITS RESULT before replying — it BLOCKS while the loop drives the device to completion. Recommended: tell the user 'Driving your phone now — I'll report back', then wait for the tool result. High-consequence actions (send SMS/email, payments, deletions) and passwords are gated ON THE DEVICE (the user confirms/enters them). Returns a natural-language summary of what was done, or a structured error (device offline / stopped on the device / accessibility off / lost contact / timed out) so you can retry or stop.

task.description: The natural-language task to perform on the device, e.g. 'open Google Maps and search for coffee near me' or 'what's on my screen right now?'.

device.description: Optional target device name (tailnet hostname or MagicDNS name) — targets ANY device on the tailnet. Omit to use origin-aware routing: the device the request came from, else the operator's designated PRIMARY device. Naming a device that is not this operator's origin is never a silent retarget — it must be an explicit target here.

provider.description: Optional frontier brain to drive the device. Omit to use the target device's configured default_provider, else the box default. 'gemini' (validated Android CU), 'claude'/'openai' (cloud vision models, DIY-on-Android), or 'gemma' (the on-device model — the legacy control_phone path). An explicit value overrides the per-device default.

returns: A natural-language summary of what the frontier loop did on the device, or a structured error (error_kind: no_device / stopped / accessibility_off / lost_contact / timeout / model_error / config_error / invalid_argument / invalid_target / max_steps / loop_error), plus data.steps, data.device and data.provider.
example: control_device(task="open the camera app and take a photo")
notes: M2/M3/M7 of the frontier-driven device-control rewire: a server-side ReAct loop (Orchestrator/frontier_agent_loop.py) drives the phone's M1 endpoints (GET /stream observations + POST /action) over Tailscale with hybrid tree+screenshot grounding. Provider-agnostic (M7): gemini/claude/openai each pair a FrontierDriver with a coordinate adapter (Gemini 0-999; Anthropic/OpenAI absolute-px with screenshot downscale+rescale — frontier_grounding.py); gemma routes to the on-device control_phone path behind the same seam. Provider resolves as explicit param → device default_provider (M3 registry) → [computer_use] frontier_provider. Device resolution is origin-aware (mesh.resolve_device): explicit device → any tailnet node; else the origin device (must belong to this operator — never silent-retarget); else the operator's primary device. See docs/plans/2026-06-30-android-control-frontier-driven-plan.md (M7).
